spec_match.rationale: No-developer-visible-change feature — by definition the API doesn't expose the new behaviour, so the demo can only probe indirectly. Honest about the limit but doesn't fully match the spec's framing.

interactive.rationale: 3 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects / 0 ranges driving live JS — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates the feature-index blurb's key promise (overlap on behavior, overscroll).

edge_cases.rationale: Demo runs the happy path; no visible feature detection, error handling, or no-support fallback in the script.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/5099117340655616 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: all three values demonstrated (auto/contain/none)
openQuestions.detail: The respect-overscroll-behavior-for-keyboard-scrolls feature's headline framing — Keyboard scrolling now respects overscroll-behavior (auto/contain/none), like mouse and touch already do — calls out "all three values demonstrated (auto/contain/none)". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: modal trap / focus interactions
openQuestions.detail: The respect-overscroll-behavior-for-keyboard-scrolls feature's headline framing — Keyboard scrolling now respects overscroll-behavior (auto/contain/none), like mouse and touch already do — calls out "modal trap / focus interactions". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

summary: kbd-overscroll under respect-overscroll-behavior-for-keyboard-scrolls: 4 pass / 2 partial / 0 fail. Strong interactive surface, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
